exact.all_order_interval: Q_(m,n)(lambda)>0 for every integer m>=1,n>=0 and -100<=lambda<=0
exact.arbitrary_column_consequence: all-order contiguous interval positivity => all-order consecutive-row arbitrary-column signed-Hankel positivity
exact.bridge_boundary: all-order shifted signed-Hankel positivity does not by itself prove PF-infinity of every binomially weighted Jensen window
exact.candidate_endpoint: Q_(m,n)(-100)>0 for every integer m>=10 and n>=0
exact.endpoint_interval_equivalence: [Q_(m,n)(-100)>0 for every m>=10,n>=0] iff [Q_(m,n)(lambda)>0 for every m>=10,n>=0,-100<=lambda<=0]
exact.fixed_order_tail: for every fixed m exists N_m such that for every n>=N_m and -100<=lambda<=0, Q_(m,n)(lambda)>0
exact.single_layer_implication: [Q_(k,n)>0 on the heat interval for every k<m,n and Q_(m,n)(-100)>0 for every n] => [Q_(m,n)(lambda)>0 for every n and -100<=lambda<=0]

proof_boundary: This artifact proves the arbitrary-order determinant identities, single-layer tail propagation, induction over compound order, and the endpoint/heat-interval equivalence. The all-order positivity antecedent is false at order ten. This does not prove the Jensen-window PF bridge, PF-infinity, Jensen hyperbolicity, RH, or Lambda<=0.

rows.claim: Use one signed coordinate at every compound order, with the empty minor normalized to one.
rows.proof_boundary: Definition only; no sign is asserted.

rows.claim: Every fixed order has a heat-uniform eventual positive signed tail, although its threshold may depend on the order.

source.status: exact all-fixed-order graded-kernel leading determinant lemma and compact-uniform eventual signed tails; no all-shift theorem

rows.formula: for every fixed m exists N_m such that for every n>=N_m and -100<=lambda<=0, Q_(m,n)(lambda)>0
rows.proof_boundary: The quantifier is for every m there exists N_m, not one N uniform in m.

rows.claim: Desnanot-Jacobi converts each new endpoint layer into strict log-concavity of the preceding positive layer.
rows.proof_boundary: The sign follows from one exact orientation-parity flip.

rows.claim: Multilinearity and duplicate-column/row cancellation give the affine Hankel heat derivative at arbitrary order.
rows.proof_boundary: Only the final shifted column and final shifted row survive.

rows.claim: The adjacent flag-Plucker relation converts the affine derivative into a nearest-neighbor system.

diagnostics.scope: Independent finite symbolic specializations; arbitrary-order validity comes from the determinant cancellation, Desnanot-Jacobi, and flag Plucker derivations recorded in the theorem rows.

rows.proof_boundary: Apply the three-term flag relation to C_0,...,C_m and their first m-1 rows.

rows.claim: Whenever the preceding signed layer is positive, the order-m flow is cooperative in the forward shift direction.
rows.proof_boundary: For m>=2; c_(m,n)>0 and the lower-layer quotient is positive.

rows.claim: At one fixed order, endpoint entry and the order-dependent eventual tail propagate positivity to the whole heat interval.
rows.formula: [Q_(k,n)>0 on the heat interval for every k<m,n and Q_(m,n)(-100)>0 for every n] => [Q_(m,n)(lambda)>0 for every n and -100<=lambda<=0]; Q_(m,n)(lambda)=E_(m,n)(lambda)*(Q_(m,n)(-100)+integral_(-100)^lambda E_(m,n)(s)^(-1)*a_(m,n)(s)*Q_(m,n+1)(s)ds), E_(m,n)>0
rows.proof_boundary: Fix m first, choose N_m, and descend only through the finite shifts N_m-1,...,0.

rows.claim: The rigorous fixed-order programme supplies the induction base through order nine.

source.status: signed contiguous and arbitrary-column order-nine theorem on -100<=lambda<=0

rows.proof_boundary: No order at least ten is imported.

rows.claim: Ordinary induction over m makes the remaining all-order heat theorem equivalent to one static endpoint hierarchy.
rows.formula: [Q_(m,n)(-100)>0 for every m>=10,n>=0] iff [Q_(m,n)(lambda)>0 for every m>=10,n>=0,-100<=lambda<=0]
rows.proof_boundary: The reverse implication is restriction to lambda=-100; the forward implication iterates the single-layer lemma.

rows.claim: After contiguous completion at every finite order, the initial-minor theorem transfers the result to arbitrary columns pointwise in lambda.

source.status: exact arbitrary-column reshaped-Hankel order-four theorem at lambda zero

rows.formula: all-order contiguous interval positivity => all-order consecutive-row arbitrary-column signed-Hankel positivity
rows.proof_boundary: For each finite m separately; no infinite determinant is invoked.

rows.claim: The conditional all-order theorem is equivalently an infinite strict log-concavity hierarchy in the positive signed coordinates.
rows.proof_boundary: This is an exact reformulation of the candidate antecedent; the next row records that the antecedent is false.

rows.claim: The candidate all-order signed endpoint hierarchy fails at its first uncompleted order.

source.status: rigorous first-open-order endpoint counterexample rejecting the all-order deep rectangular hierarchy

rows.formula: Q_(m,n)(-100)>0 for every integer m>=10 and n>=0; but Q_(10,n)(-100)<0 for n=0,1,2,3
rows.proof_boundary: Rejects the all-order signed-Hankel antecedent for the actual endpoint sequence, not the exact conditional heat algebra or RH.

rows.claim: The Jensen-window PF problem remains separate and now requires an antecedent weaker than all-shift all-order signed-Hankel positivity.
rows.formula: all-order shifted signed-Hankel positivity does not by itself prove PF-infinity of every binomially weighted Jensen window
rows.proof_boundary: Blocks promotion of signed-Hankel sign regularity directly to RH.

source_contract.fixed_order_column_transfer: completed signed contiguous layers through each fixed m imply all consecutive-row arbitrary-column signed minors through m
source_contract.fixed_order_tail: for every fixed m there exists N_m such that Q_(m,n)(lambda)>0 for n>=N_m and -100<=lambda<=0

sources.status: exact all-fixed-order graded-kernel leading determinant lemma and compact-uniform eventual signed tails; no all-shift theorem
exact uniform eventual signed order-six tail and conditional forward reduction with one open lambda=-100 entry
signed contiguous and arbitrary-column order-nine theorem on -100<=lambda<=0
exact arbitrary-column reshaped-Hankel order-four theorem at lambda zero
rigorous first-open-order endpoint counterexample rejecting the all-order deep rectangular hierarchy

status: exact all-order endpoint-to-heat equivalence with a completed base through order nine and the candidate endpoint hierarchy rejected by the order-ten counterexample

symbolic_identity_audit.scope: Independent finite symbolic specializations; arbitrary-order validity comes from the determinant cancellation, Desnanot-Jacobi, and flag Plucker derivations recorded in the theorem rows.
